# UC-22.13.69 · Transmission Physical Security Plan Control Checklist Status

## Description

We track periodic checklist completions for transmission station physical security plan controls such as lighting, fencing, and cameras.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes cip014_control_check, cip014_control_check`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Encode ninety-day inspection default (tune per plan). (2) Mobile form integration to Splunk via HEC. (3) Escalate failed controls to field ops. (4) Photo evidence stored outside Splunk with URL field. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip014_control_check`, `sourcetype=cip014_control_check``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip014_control_check`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Encode ninety-day inspection default (tune per plan). (2) Mobile form integration to Splunk via HEC. (3) Escalate failed controls to field ops. (4) Photo evidence stored outside Splunk with URL field.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip014_control_check earliest=-400d
| eval performed=strptime(performed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()-performed>7776000,1,0)
| where overdue=1 AND result!="pass"
| table station_id, control_id, performed_date, result, owner
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transmission Physical Security Plan Control Checklist Status (CIP-014-3 R2 Part 2.1)** — We track periodic checklist completions for transmission station physical security plan controls such as lighting, fencing, and cameras.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip014_control_check`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: cip014_control_check. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=cip014_control_check, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **performed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where overdue=1 AND result!="pass"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: control_id, overdue, owner, performed, performed_date, result, station_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed/overdue), Column chart (by control), Single value (stations)

## SPL

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip014_control_check earliest=-400d
| eval performed=strptime(performed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()-performed>7776000,1,0)
| where overdue=1 AND result!="pass"
| table station_id, control_id, performed_date, result, owner
```
